title: Drift Correction

content.question: What's your weekly reset ritual? Do you do it?
sop: Write down the original standard for one key process (response time, output quality, pricing discipline).
Rate your current Deviation 1-10 from that original standard (see calculation, page 4)
Set your correction Frequency, weekly for fast-moving areas, monthly for slower ones (see calculation, page 4)
Realign the process back to standard at every scheduled check, even by a small amount.
Track whether Deviation is shrinking or growing over time, if it's growing, increase Frequency.
questions: What's your weekly reset ritual? Do you do it?
What standard did you quietly lower this month without deciding to?
How would you know if a system had drifted if you never compared it back to the original?
content.equation_example: Gap Closed per fix rated 1-10 (how far you pull back toward standard): 6. Frequency: 4 corrections per month. Correction = 6 × 4 = 24. Higher/rising means you're actively catching and closing drift as it appears; lower means drift is accumulating faster than you address it.
content.closing_statement: A standard you never re-check has already started slipping.

caption:
Principle Nº 148: Drift Correction

Everything drifts, correction required.

Ask yourself: What's your weekly reset ritual? Do you do it?
